categories.name: Linear Algebra & Differential Equations
categories.description: Choose one linear algebra and differential equations sequence. AM 10 is Engineering Math Methods I; AM 20 is Engineering Math Methods II. MATH 21 is Linear Algebra; MATH 24 is Ordinary Differential Equations.

categories.name: Multivariate Calculus
categories.description: Choose one multivariate/vector calculus course. AM 30 is Multivariate Calculus for Engineers; MATH 23A is Vector Calculus.

categories.name: Probability & Statistics
categories.description: Choose one probability and statistics course.

categories.name: Physics
categories.description: Required physics courses. PHYS 5A/L is Mechanics; PHYS 5C/N is Electricity & Magnetism. Honors alternatives PHYS 15A and PHYS 15C may substitute (consult BE advising).

categories.name: Engineering Mechanics
categories.description: ECE 9: Statics and Mechanics of Materials. Prerequisites: PHYS 5A/L and MATH 21 or AM 10.

categories.name: Discrete Mathematics
categories.description: CSE 16: Discrete Mathematics. Prerequisite: MATH 19A or 19B.

categories.name: Lower Division Programming
categories.description: Required lower-division programming and computer systems courses. CSE 20 is Beginning Programming in Python. CSE 30 is Programming Abstractions: Python & C. CSE 12 is Computer Systems and Assembly Language. ECE 13 is Computer Systems and C Programming. CSE 20 can be bypassed via the CSE 20 testout.

categories.name: Logic Design
categories.description: CSE 100/L: Logic Design. Prerequisite: CSE 12. Requires declared major status.

categories.name: Data Structures & Algorithms
categories.description: CSE 101: Introduction to Data Structures and Algorithms. Prerequisites: CSE 12, ECE 13, CSE 16, CSE 30, and MATH 19B.

categories.name: Electronics
categories.description: ECE 101/L: Electronic Circuits. Prerequisites: PHYS 5C/N and MATH 24 or PHYS 116A, or previous or concurrent enrollment in AM 20.

categories.name: Robotics Core
categories.description: Required upper-division robotics courses. ECE 10: Fundamentals of Robotics (Kinematics & Dynamics). ECE 118: Intro to Mechatronics. ECE 121: Microcontroller System Design. ECE 103/L: Signals & Systems. ECE 167: Sensing & Sensor Technologies.

categories.name: Advanced Robotics Elective
categories.description: Choose 1 advanced robotics elective. ECE 141 (Adv. Feedback Control Systems) and ECE 167 are highly recommended but not required for this slot. A second advanced elective may also be used to satisfy the Robotics Elective requirement.

categories.name: Robotics Elective
categories.description: Choose 1 robotics elective from the approved list. A second advanced elective course may also satisfy this requirement. See https://undergrad.engineering.ucsc.edu/curriculum-charts/2025-2026/robotics-engineering-electives-2025-2026/ for the full list.

categories.name: Capstone (DC)

groups.label: Capstone Project I, II, & III (10 credits)

categories.description: Choose one capstone option. Both options satisfy the Disciplinary Communication (DC) requirement. Prerequisites for both: CSE 100/L, ECE 118, ECE 121, and completion of the Entry Level Writing and Composition requirements. Students interested in ECE 195 should consult BE undergrad advising for additional requirements.
